notes: Evals grounded in the real ContactMatchingService cluster in the openregister app — see examples/openregister-contact-matching.md. Scenarios exercise the two flag paths (--extend, --cluster), the model guard, and the 5-REQ cap that prevents cluster sprawl.

evals.description: Block on Haiku and instruct the user to switch models
evals.expected_output: Skill stops immediately without drafting REQs
expectations: Detects active model is Haiku
Stops immediately and does not read any code
Explicitly tells the user to switch to Sonnet or Opus
Does not create a ghost change scaffold
Does not touch the git tree

evals.description: --cluster flow creates a brand-new capability spec with retrofit: true
evals.setup: openregister/openspec/coverage-report.json < 24h old, bucket_2b['contact-matching'] lists 5 public + 5 private methods on ContactMatchingService. No specs/contact-matching/ exists. Working tree clean. Active model is Sonnet.
evals.expected_output: Ghost change retrofit-contact-matching-<date> with a full spec, tasks all [x], annotations on every method, archived, PR opened.
expectations: Checks out the branch recorded in coverage-report.json.branch (not assumed to be development)
Creates branch retrofit/reverse-spec-openregister-contact-matching-<date>
Reads the listed methods to understand observed behavior (inputs, outputs, pre/postconditions, failure modes)
Silently reads org-wide ADRs and openregister's repo-specific ADRs + sibling specs
Drafts exactly 5 REQs — one per distinct observable behavior (email, name, organization, orchestrated, cache invalidation)
Writes specs/contact-matching/spec.md with frontmatter 'retrofit: true'
Writes tasks.md with one [x] task per REQ
Fills in proposal.md with Affected code units + Approach sections
Annotates each method inline with @spec pointing at tasks.md#task-N — does NOT invoke /opsx-annotate
Runs sync_spec_content.py; stops on failure
Archives via /opsx-archive — delta merges into openspec/specs/
Appends the annotation commit SHA to .git-blame-ignore-revs
Opens a PR with retrofit + reverse-spec labels

evals.description: --extend flow appends REQs to an existing capability with retrofit_extensions frontmatter
evals.setup: openregister/openspec/coverage-report.json lists bucket_2a['audit-trail-immutable'] with 3 new observable behaviors. openspec/specs/audit-trail-immutable/spec.md already exists. Active model is Sonnet.
evals.expected_output: Ghost change contains ONLY the delta spec (new REQs), with retrofit_extensions: [...] in frontmatter. Main spec is untouched until archive.
expectations: Validates specs/audit-trail-immutable/spec.md exists before starting
Finds the next free REQ number in the capability and starts numbering from there
Writes changes/.../specs/audit-trail-immutable/spec.md containing ONLY the new REQs (delta, not full spec)
Delta frontmatter carries retrofit_extensions: [REQ-NNN, ...] listing the added REQ IDs
Does NOT mint a new capability or touch other specs
Tasks map each new REQ to its implementing methods
Archive step merges the delta into the existing main spec
Specter sync picks up retrofit_extensions and flags the app_specs row

evals.description: Cluster with >5 distinct behaviors is split across runs, not crammed into one
evals.setup: bucket_2b['workflow-engine'] lists 12 methods across 4 files with 8 distinct observable behaviors. Active model is Sonnet.
evals.expected_output: This run drafts the first 5 REQs. The user is told to re-invoke for the remaining behaviors. No REQ inflation, no collapsed distinct behaviors.
expectations: Caps this run at 5 REQs
Picks the 5 most cohesive observable behaviors — does not pick randomly
Summary block names the remaining behaviors as 'Next: run again for {names}'
Does NOT create a second ghost change in the same run
Does NOT collapse 8 behaviors into 5 by merging distinct ones

evals.description: Refuses to run against a coverage report older than 24h
evals.setup: coverage-report.json exists but mtime is 36h old
evals.expected_output: Skill stops and asks the user to re-run /opsx-coverage-scan
expectations: Reads coverage-report.json mtime
Detects staleness (>24h)
Stops before reading any code
Tells the user to re-run /opsx-coverage-scan openregister
Does not create a branch or ghost change

should_trigger: reverse-spec the contact-matching cluster in openregister
draft a retrofit spec from the observed code in ContactMatchingService
run opsx-reverse-spec on openregister for the audit-trail-immutable extension
reverse engineer a spec for this Bucket 2 cluster
let's retrofit a spec for the contact-matching service
use /opsx-reverse-spec to handle Bucket 2b cluster workflow-engine
create a ghost change with a spec delta for the new behaviors in AuditHashService
I want to reverse-spec one cluster from the latest coverage report
retrofit spec draft for cluster contact-matching
annotate these new methods and draft REQs for them
should_not_trigger: run the coverage scan on openregister
apply the openspec change to openregister
archive this change once it merges
propose a new openspec change for a brand new feature
show me the Bucket 2 entries in the report
